title: gagea reticulata subsp. fibrosa
intro: Gagea reticulata subsp. fibrosa, also known as the 'net-leaved lily' or 'fibrous marsh-marigold', is a type of flowering plant belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Gagea reticulata subsp. fibrosa include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Gagea reticulata subsp. fibrosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
